```json
{
  "analysis": "The fix is confirmed correct. Bug: in pool_sweep() in runtime/shared_heap.c, the loop step `p += Whsize_hd(hd)` was incorrect. Pool blocks are aligned to the sizeclass slot width `wh`, so the loop should advance by `wh` each iteration. `Whsize_hd(hd)` computes `Wosize_hd(hd) + 1`, which for live objects can be smaller than `wh` (due to sizeclass padding), causing pointer misalignment and corruption. For free blocks with RLE count > 0, the existing `p += wh * Wosize_hd(hd)` skip plus the (now fixed) `p += wh` correctly advance past all contiguous free blocks. Fix: changed `p += Whsize_hd(hd)` to `p += wh`. The compiler built and bootstrapped successfully, and all 40 basic tests passed with 0 failures.",
  "plan": "Task complete. The single-line fix in runtime/shared_heap.c resolves the GC sweeping bug.",
  "commands": [],
  "task_complete": true
}
```
